schema.productDescription: CRADYL is a movable docking station that autonomously moves a solar panel cleaning robot from one row to another, enabling multi-row cleaning with a single robot on scattered plants.
schema.offersPrice: Coming soon — contact for early access

hero.title: CRADYL — Autonomous Row-Transfer Docking
hero.subtitle: A movable docking station that carries your solar panel cleaning robot from one row to the next autonomously — so a single robot can clean multiple rows across scattered, non-contiguous plant layouts.
hero.imgAlt: Taypro CRADYL autonomous row-transfer docking station for solar cleaning robots — coming soon

intro.title: Unlock multi-row coverage without multiplying robot CAPEX
intro.p1: Scattered utility blocks and irregular row spacing force operators to choose between deploying multiple robots or accepting manual transfers between arrays. CRADYL automates that gap: a self-propelled docking platform receives the cleaning robot, navigates to the next row, and redeploys it — hands-free.
intro.p2: CRADYL is designed to pair with Taypro cleaning robots already in the field. Register your plant layout to join early pilot discussions and integration planning with our robotics team.

0.title: Autonomous row-to-row transfer
0.body: Moves one solar panel cleaning robot between rows without crew lift-and-shift — reducing labour and transfer downtime between blocks.

1.title: Single-robot, multi-row coverage
1.body: Stretch one robot across more rows per shift on plants where buying a robot per contiguous block is not economical.

2.title: Built for scattered layouts
2.body: Engineered for plants with gaps, roads, drains, or non-uniform row spacing between arrays — common across Indian utility portfolios.

3.title: Fleet-ready integration
3.body: Planned compatibility with NECTYR for transfer status, route planning, and support workflows alongside existing robots.

0.title: Scattered utility blocks
0.body: Multi-block plants where rows are separated by roads, drainage, or buffer land and one robot cannot traverse continuously.

1.title: Semi-automatic robot fleets
1.body: Sites running HELYX or similar pick-and-place robots that need faster inter-row movement without adding headcount.

2.title: CAPEX optimisation programmes
2.body: Owners who want autonomous cleaning coverage but need to defer buying additional robots until transfer automation proves ROI.

notify.title: Get notified when CRADYL launches

0.label: HELYX — Semi-automatic robot
0.description: Pick-and-place waterless cleaning for scattered blocks — a natural partner for CRADYL transfers.

1.label: GLYDE — Automatic robot
1.description: Fully autonomous cleaning for contiguous fixed-tilt rows before CRADYL extends reach across gaps.

CradylPage.footerNote: CRADYL is under active development. Robot compatibility, specifications, and availability dates may change before launch.
